post.description:
Mint a long-lived local operator JWT for the dedicated-PC cockpit.

This is deliberately unavailable in `strict`/guarded modes. The single-PC
profile trades auth friction for operational autonomy, but the endpoint
must still be impossible to accidentally expose in the standard profile.

get.description:
Ops-facing snapshot of the live runtime: versions and policy defaults.

Operators read this to confirm the API really restarted with the policy
flags they expect (encryption required, four-eyes, approval TTL, research
persistence backend). Cheaper than a full health dashboard and safe to
poll from the operator console.

No-friction readiness diagnostic for the active operator profile.

Returns a punch-list of `.env` flags that block capabilities the profile
would otherwise enable. The diagnostic is informational — no flag is ever
auto-flipped: the operator opts in explicitly. (Fase 72 A.)

List configured MCP servers, their connection status and tool count.

Calls each server's `tools/list` RPC so the report reflects real
connectivity, not just the parsed `.env`. Servers that fail report
`connected=false` + a short error string — no exception escapes the
handler. (Fase 73 D.)

Live inventory of operator-supplied credentials.

Admin-only because it lists capability gates the operator hasn't yet
activated. Values are NEVER returned — only booleans, the capability the
credential unlocks, and a deterministic `how_to_obtain` pointer so the
operator can finish bootstrap from this single response.

post.description:
Operator-triggered LIVE health check.

Unlike `/health/dashboard` (passive, never spends), this runs real probes
against the spend/latency-bearing components — a tiny LLM completion, a real
embedding, and an IMAP login. It exists so the operator can turn the honest
`configured` overall status into a verified `ok` on demand. See AUDIT-2026-B.

SSE stream of LangGraph node updates for incremental UX feedback.

Each `data:` line is a JSON object with shape:
  {"event": "<event_name>", "thread_id": "...", "payload": {...}}

Events emitted:
  * `thread_started`        — after thread_id resolution.
  * `node_update`           — one per node completion (router, retrieve_context,
                               research/legal/comm/social, human_review, final_response).
  * `final_response`        — assistant message + active_route + pending_human_review.
  * `interrupt`             — emitted instead of final_response when the graph paused
                               for human approval; client should call `/threads/{id}/resume`.
  * `error`                 — emitted on any uncaught exception. Generator still terminates.
  * `done`                  — last event, signals stream end.

schema.title: Response Openshell Status Sandbox Openshell Status Get
Response Get Document Analysis Result Document Analysis  Task Id  Get
Response List Deepagent Skills Deepagents Skills Get

get.description: Return the metadata + raw `SKILL.md` content for one skill.

schema.title: Response List Document Chunks Documents  Document Id  Chunks Get

get.description:
List recent LangGraph threads.

Combines two signals:
  1. The `langgraph` `checkpoints` table (if `PostgresSaver` is active) —
     this captures every chat thread, including ones that never enqueued a
     Celery job.
  2. `jobs.metadata_json.thread_id` — captures DeepAgent / DocumentAnalysis
     tasks even when the in-memory checkpointer is in use.

Results are deduped by `thread_id` and sorted newest first.

schema.title: Response List Recent Threads Threads Get
Response List Deepagent Memory Deepagents Memory Get
Response List Deepagent Memory Proposals Deepagents Memory Proposals Get

post.description: Admin-gated: memory mutations permanently shape future agent runs.

schema.title: Response Approve Deepagent Memory Proposal Deepagents Memory Proposals  Proposal Id  Approve Post
Response Export Deepagent Memory Deepagents Memory Export Post

post.description: Persists a short-lived episodic memory tied to this user without a proposal round-trip.

schema.title: Response Append Episodic Memory Deepagents Memory Episodic Post
Response List Assist Tasks Assist Tasks Get
Response List Assist Notes Assist Notes Get
Response Search Assist Notes Assist Notes Search Get
Response Get Job Events Jobs  Job Id  Events Get

get.description: Recent jobs, newest first. Filter by `job_type` and/or `status`.

post.description: Cancel a job and revoke its Celery task when the task id is known.

schema.title: Response List Audit Events Audit Events Get

get.description: Non-secret configuration view for the operator panel.
Current external-action posture without exposing secrets.

schema.title: Response Action Capabilities Actions Capabilities Get
Response List Action Requests Actions Requests Get

get.description:
Export an ActionRequest as a portable `workflow.v1` document.

The export uses the redacted payload — encrypted secrets stay server-side.
Operators can edit the document and re-submit via
`POST /actions/requests/from-workflow` to clone the plan.

post.description:
Re-create an ActionRequest from a `workflow.v1` document.

The importer routes the document through the same `create_*_request`
carrils the standard endpoints use, so allow-lists, approval gating,
idempotency dedup and payload encryption all apply unchanged.

schema.title: Response Calendar Events Actions Calendar Events Post

post.description:
Preview-only Calendar create surface.

Real writes must use `/actions/calendar/events/request`, so every mutation
is backed by `ActionRequest`, `HumanApproval`, Celery dispatch and audit.

schema.title: Response Drive Files Actions Drive Files Post

post.description:
Preview-only Drive upload surface.

Real uploads must use `/actions/drive/files/upload/request`, so every file
mutation is backed by `ActionRequest`, `HumanApproval`, Celery dispatch and audit.

schema.title: Response Dispatch Personal Mail Sync Mail Sync Dispatch Post
Response Dispatch Personal Mail Digest Mail Digest Dispatch Post
Response List Personal Mail Messages Mail Messages Get

post.description: Persist an interactive browsing plan (multi-step with optional vision).

Dispatch the per-agent memory consolidation Celery task immediately.

Admin-gated: the task rewrites long-term agent memory; only operators with
admin role or `ADMIN_USER_IDS` membership should be able to trigger it
out-of-band.

schema.title: Response Trigger Memory Consolidation Deepagents Memory Consolidate Run Post

get.description:
Fase 78 (Fase A): proposals with ``kind == "procedure"`` only.

Thin filter on top of `/deepagents/memory/proposals` so the Memory
UI can render the new ``Recetas`` section without spelunking through
a generic list of all kinds.

schema.title: Response List Recipe Proposals Deepagents Memory Recipes Get

post.description:
Admin-gated: run the recipe extractor immediately (Fase 78).

Useful for operator demos and when the operator just finished a
long-running job and wants the proposal to show up without waiting
for the next 30-minute beat tick.

schema.title: Response Trigger Recipe Extraction Deepagents Memory Recipes Extract Now Post

get.description:
Fase 79.3 (Fase D): proposals with ``kind == "warning"`` only.

The Memory UI uses this to surface failure-recovery patterns the
scanner found in past jobs. Auto-promoted warnings show up here as
``status="applied"`` so the operator can still review and archive
them if they turn out to be noise.

schema.title: Response List Warning Proposals Deepagents Memory Warnings Get

post.description:
Admin-gated: run the failure-postmortem scanner immediately (Fase 79.3).

The scanner walks recently completed jobs for tool_failed → tool_succeeded
recovery pairs and emits ``warning`` proposals. Same approval gate as
the recipe extractor.

schema.title: Response Trigger Failure Postmortem Scan Deepagents Memory Warnings Scan Now Post

get.description:
Fase 79.4: read-side endpoint for the tool effectiveness scorecard.

Returns recent rollup rows sorted by (period_start DESC, reliability
DESC). The UI tab uses this to surface high-confidence + low-confidence
tools per agent role. No PII — just counters and the derived score.

schema.title: Response List Tool Scorecard Deepagents Learning Tool Scorecard Get

post.description:
Admin-gated: aggregate the previous day's tool events into the rollup
table immediately (Fase 79.4). Useful for the operator after a busy day
to see the scorecard without waiting for the 04:15 UTC beat tick.

schema.title: Response Trigger Tool Scorecard Aggregation Deepagents Learning Tool Scorecard Aggregate Now Post

get.description:
Fase 80 (Fase B): proposals carrying ``metadata.skill_promotion``.

The Memory UI uses this to surface procedures the skill promoter has
decided are ready to become first-class YAML skills. Pending entries
need an explicit operator approval; auto-promotion of executable
behaviour is forbidden by §7 of AGENT_LEARNING_PLAN.md.

schema.title: Response List Skill Promotion Proposals Endpoint Deepagents Learning Skill Promotions Get

post.description:
Admin-gated: run the skill promoter immediately (Fase 80). Also
triggers the post-promotion rollback safety pass.

schema.title: Response Trigger Skill Promoter Deepagents Learning Skill Promotions Evaluate Now Post

post.description:
Materialise an approved skill promotion proposal as a YAML skill.

Admin-gated. Writes ``storage/deepagents/skills/user/_auto/<slug>/SKILL.md``
and emits a derived ``DeepAgentMemoryRecord`` so the skill is
discoverable by future agent builds. Idempotent: re-running on an
already-applied proposal returns the same descriptor.

schema.title: Response Approve Skill Promotion Deepagents Learning Skill Promotions  Proposal Id  Approve Post

get.description:
Fase 81 (Fase E): nightly reflection proposals with evidence quotes.

The UI uses ``evidence_quotes`` to render each proposal alongside the
literal substring of the conversation that justified it — so the
operator never has to take the agent's word for the inference.

schema.title: Response List Reflection Proposals Deepagents Learning Reflection Get

post.description:
Admin-gated: run the nightly reflection immediately (Fase 81).

Honours the auto-disable circuit breaker: if the operator has
rejected too many recent proposals, the task returns early.

schema.title: Response Trigger Nightly Reflection Deepagents Learning Reflection Run Now Post

get.description: Operational view of every controlled agent with config + recent activity.

schema.title: Response Langsmith Projects Langsmith Projects Get
Response Langsmith Runs Langsmith Runs Get
Response List Research Runs Research Runs Get

get.description:
SSE stream of `ResearchEvent`s for a single research run.

The endpoint emits every event already produced by the run (so a client
that connects late still sees the full history), then keeps streaming
new events until the run reaches a terminal state. The final frame is
a `snapshot` event carrying the full `ResearchRunView` so the UI does
not need a separate GET to render the result.

post.description:
Plan a code build and persist it as a waiting-approval job.

NO coding agent runs and NO token is spent here. The operator must
approve the returned HumanApproval (which carries the plan + budget
estimate) before the build is dispatched.

get.description:
SSE stream of the build's JobEvents until the job reaches a terminal state.

The director persists one JobEvent per build event, so this replays the
full history then tails new events. The final frame is a `snapshot` with
the job status + result.

Download the packaged tar.gz of a completed build.

The artifact path is resolved from job metadata and re-validated to be
inside `document_output_root/code_builds/` so a tampered metadata value
cannot escape the output root.

AdapterPreference.title: AdapterPreference
AdapterPreference.description:
Operator's preference for which adapter handles which role.

The director keeps four conceptual roles. Each can be pinned to an
adapter+model pair. Roles not pinned fall back to the adapter listed in
`default_adapter`.

BrowserStep.title: BrowserStep
BrowserStep.description:
One step in a `browser_interactive` plan.

- `navigate`: load a NEW url (must pass the domain allow-list)
- `click`: click a CSS selector
- `fill`: type `value` into a CSS selector
- `scroll`: scroll the page by `value` pixels (default 600)
- `wait`: wait `value` milliseconds (capped to 10s)
- `screenshot`: capture a PNG and persist it inside BROWSER_SCREENSHOT_DIR
- `analyze`: capture a screenshot then call the multimodal LLM with `prompt`

BudgetSpec.title: BudgetSpec
BudgetSpec.description:
Hard limits the director honours during a build.

Hitting any limit stops the build with status=`partial` and emits a
`budget_exceeded` event. The operator can then decide whether to extend
or abandon.

BuildPlan.title: BuildPlan
BuildPlan.description: Director's decomposition. Operator approves THIS before any token is spent.

detail.title: Detail
detail.default: Build planned. Approve the linked HumanApproval to start it; no coding agent runs until then.

CodeBuildRequest.title: CodeBuildRequest
CodeBuildRequest.description:
High-level user-facing build spec.

The director consumes this to produce a `BuildPlan`, which the operator
then approves before any tokens are spent.

EpisodicMemoryRequest.title: EpisodicMemoryRequest
EpisodicMemoryRequest.description:
Registro autorizado de un hecho episódico para el usuario (cronología corta/medio plazo).

Sirve como base temporal hasta integrar extracción automática desde trabajos y Telegram.

require_human_approval_for_external_acti.title: Require Human Approval For External Actions

env_var.title: Env Var
env_var.description: Exact env variable to set in .env

capability.title: Capability
capability.description: What you get by flipping this

severity.title: Severity
severity.description: `info` = expected for this profile (no action). `suggestion` = capability available but the operator hasn't opted in. `warning` = the current value contradicts the profile's intent.

SubtaskSpec.title: SubtaskSpec
SubtaskSpec.description: One unit of work the director will dispatch to an adapter.

require_human_approval_for_external_acti.title: Require Human Approval For External Actions

preview.title: Preview
preview.description: Optional read-only preview snapshot for the operator UI.

WorkflowDocument.title: WorkflowDocument
WorkflowDocument.description:
Versioned, declarative export of an ActionRequest plan.

Stable on-disk format so operators can clone, edit, version-control and
re-submit plans. The redacted payload is the public surface; if the
original request had executable secrets (encrypted at rest), they are
intentionally NOT included — the importer recomputes a fresh payload from
user-supplied fields.

Schema versioning: `workflow_version` starts at `1.0`. Future breaking
changes bump the major; additive changes bump the minor.

WorkflowSource.title: WorkflowSource
WorkflowSource.description:
Provenance of a workflow document.

Optional; lets the importer trace a `workflow.v1` JSON back to the original
`ActionRequest` and the operator that exported it.
